title: Ghana Card Application Form 1-A (Form One)
description: Ghana's national form for applying for a Ghana Card (national identity card) — "Form One", printed as made under "regulation 3(1)" (the form's own header citation; the specific regulations instrument is not further identified on the form itself), and administered by the National Identification Authority (NIA), a statutory body established by the National Identification Authority Act, 2006 (Act 707). The form serves citizens, permanently resident non-citizens, persons with a residence permit, and refugees alike, and covers first-time issuance as well as update, replacement, and re-issue of a previously issued card. Opens Ghana as the registry's 41st jurisdiction, via the National ID & Civic Documents vertical. This schema models the form's full applicant-facing content across both pages: applicant/request type, personal particulars (name, sex, marital status, height, colour of eyes/hair, disability code, level of education), birth/nationality particulars and place of birth/hometown, occupation, residential address, up to 14 languages spoken, the applicant's parentage (father's and mother's particulars, including whether each is alive), next of kin, a bounded list of up to 5 spouses (the form's own footnote directs applicants with more spouses to a separate "Spouses Form"), non-citizen-only particulars (residence-permit and employer details), the verification document presented, dual-citizenship-only particulars, contact numbers and email, a set of institutional ID numbers (SSNIT, Voter ID, Passport, National Health Insurance, Driver's Licence, Tax Identification Number), and the applicant's declaration. It deliberately does NOT model three widgets that are populated by NIA staff during in-person registration rather than supplied by the applicant (the "MRW Number", "Registration Centre Number", and "Interviewer NID No." fields printed in the form's header strip), nor the raw signature/thumbprint capture widget — consistent with this registry's general practice of excluding staff-populated and raw-biometric-capture widgets. Applying for a Ghana Card requires in-person attendance at an NIA registration centre for biometric capture; this schema does not submit the application itself, and the live source (nia.gov.gh) is always authoritative. GovSchema is independent and is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or operated by the Republic of Ghana or the National Identification Authority.

source.documentRef: Form One (regulation 3(1)), "National Identity Card Application Form"

verification.notes: GovSchema Standard Research cycle GOV-2510 (child of GOV-2507). Opens Ghana's National ID & Civic Documents vertical (1 of 6), via NIA's 'Form One' (regulation 3(1)), the National Identity Card Application Form. Fetched directly from nia.gov.gh (HTTP 200, application/pdf, 498,907 bytes, sha256:3f401681db2e6d1f39fcc16b78c6b69a71d81a43075789513de29d00593bc5fa, Last-Modified 2026-02-16), re-confirmed live and unauthenticated at verification time. The GOV-2510 issue's inherited claim of a 'byte-identical mirror at fims.org.gh' did not hold up on independent re-check: that path 404s, and fims.org.gh in fact hosts a different NIA form entirely (the Non-Citizen/Refugee Ghana Card application), not a mirror of this schema's source PDF — disclosed as a correction rather than repeated; the issue's 129-widget field-count claim, by contrast, did independently reproduce exactly against this schema's own from-scratch pdfjs-dist extraction (84 widgets page 1, 45 page 2). Unlike several other jurisdictions' forms in this registry, most widgets carry descriptive, self-documenting internal field names rather than generic XFA tokens, though coordinate-correlation against getTextContent() was still needed for repeated generic names (Village/Village_2.../Country/Country_2.../District/District_2...) and two ambiguous, effectively unlabelled widgets. Every Tx widget's own AcroForm charLimit was read via getFieldObjects() and used directly as validation.maxLength; this surfaced that all nationality-family fields and the 14 language-spoken blanks carry charLimit 3, and sex carries charLimit 2 — physical constraints too narrow for free text, so sex is modelled as an M/F enum (a deliberate departure from this registry's usual string-only default for unlabelled-option fields, justified here by a structural AcroForm fact rather than a visual estimate) and nationality/language fields as 3-character short-code strings. 129 widgets reconcile to 123 fields[] + 1 documents[] entry: 4 widgets (MRW Number, Registration Centre Number, Interviewer NID No., and the raw signature/thumbprint capture widget) are excluded as staff-populated or raw-biometric-capture, consistent with this registry's general practice, and the two genuine two-option AcroForm radio fields (Is Father/Mother alive) each collapse to one boolean field. Four checkbox groups (applicant type, request type, marital status, education level) are each independent Btn widgets, not true radio fields, and are modelled as N boolean fields plus an exclusivityGroups entry enforcing 'at most one true'; the form's own mandatory '*' marking ("exactly one") is disclosed in prose since exclusivityGroups only expresses the 'at most one' half. Non-citizen-only and dual-citizenship-only sections carry a visibleWhen keyed off applicantTypeCitizen, a disclosed inference from the section headings. The spouse list is bounded at 5, per the form's own printed escape hatch to a separate 'Spouses Form' for a 6th+ spouse. Confirmed via a fresh web search that the National Identification Authority Act, 2006 (Act 707) establishes NIA and that the National Identity Register Act, 2008 (Act 750) — the exact instrument the form's own declaration text cites for its Section 40 penalty — remains the governing statute, with NIA confirmed as a live, currently operating agency in 2026. See VERIFICATION.md for the full sourcing record, field-scoping decisions, and the conformance run writeup (2 valid fixtures, 0 errors each; 5 mutation-control fixtures, exactly 1 error each, including a dedicated exclusivityGroups-violation case).

fields.name: applicantTypeCitizen
fields.label: CITIZEN
fields.description: Whether the applicant is applying as a Ghanaian citizen. One of four mutually exclusive applicant-type checkboxes printed under "TYPE OF APPLICANT *"; the form requires exactly one to be ticked, but that overall choice is not machine-enforceable beyond the mutual-exclusivity captured in exclusivityGroups (see notes).

fields.name: applicantTypePermanentlyResident
fields.label: PERMANENTLY RESIDENT
fields.description: Whether the applicant is applying as a permanently resident non-citizen.
fields.sourceRef: Header — TYPE OF APPLICANT *: PERMANENTLY RESIDENT

fields.name: applicantTypePersonWithResidencePermit
fields.label: PERSON WITH RESIDENCE PERMIT
fields.description: Whether the applicant is applying as a non-citizen holding a residence permit.
fields.sourceRef: Header — TYPE OF APPLICANT *: PERSON WITH RESIDENCE PERMIT

fields.name: applicantTypeRefugee
fields.label: REFUGEE
fields.description: Whether the applicant is applying as a refugee.

fields.name: requestTypeIssuance
fields.label: Issuance
fields.description: Whether this application is for first-time issuance of a Ghana Card. One of four mutually exclusive request-type checkboxes printed under "TYPE OF REQUEST*".

fields.name: requestTypeUpdate
fields.label: Update
fields.description: Whether this application is to update particulars on an existing Ghana Card.

fields.name: requestTypeReplacement
fields.label: Replacement
fields.description: Whether this application is to replace a lost, damaged, or stolen Ghana Card.

fields.name: requestTypeReIssue
fields.label: Re-Issue
fields.description: Whether this application is to re-issue a Ghana Card.

fields.name: dateOfApplication
fields.label: Date of Application
fields.description: The date this application is submitted.

fields.name: existingNidNumber
fields.label: EXISTING NID NUMBER
fields.description: The applicant's existing Ghana Card / National ID number, when this application is an Update, Replacement, or Re-Issue rather than a first-time Issuance.

fields.name: surname
fields.label: SURNAME
fields.description: The applicant's surname.

fields.name: sex
fields.label: SEX
fields.description: The applicant's sex. The source form prints only a 2-character-wide combed text box with no printed candidate values; "M"/"F" is modelled here as the universal single-letter convention this box's width structurally accommodates (see VERIFICATION.md).

fields.name: forenames
fields.label: FORENAMES (First name and Other Names)
fields.description: The applicant's first name and other given names.

fields.name: maritalStatusSingle
fields.label: Single
fields.description: One of five mutually exclusive marital-status checkboxes printed under "Marital Status *:".

fields.name: previousOrMaidenNames
fields.label: PREVIOUS OR MAIDEN NAMES
fields.description: Any previous or maiden names the applicant has used.

fields.name: height
fields.label: Height (cm)
fields.description: The applicant's height in centimetres.

fields.name: colourOfEyes
fields.label: Colour of Eyes
fields.description: The applicant's eye colour.

fields.name: colourOfHair
fields.label: Colour of Hair
fields.description: The applicant's hair colour.

fields.name: disabilityCode
fields.label: Disability Code
fields.description: A disability code, per the form's own "Disability / Code:" label. The form does not print a lookup table of valid codes.

fields.name: educationLevelNone
fields.label: None
fields.description: One of five mutually exclusive education-level checkboxes printed under "Level of Education *:".

fields.name: birthCertificateNo
fields.label: Birth Certificate No.
fields.description: The applicant's birth certificate number.

fields.name: birthCertificateIssuedDate
fields.label: Birth Certificate Date Issued
fields.description: The date the applicant's birth certificate was issued.

fields.name: dateOfBirth
fields.label: DATE OF BIRTH
fields.description: The applicant's date of birth.

fields.name: dateOfBirthEstimated
fields.label: If Estimated Date of Birth
fields.description: Whether the date of birth entered is an estimate rather than a confirmed date.

fields.name: nationalityAtBirth
fields.label: Nationality at Birth
fields.description: The applicant's nationality at birth. The 3-character-wide combed box indicates a short nationality code is expected, not a full country name (see VERIFICATION.md).

fields.name: currentNationality
fields.label: Current Nationality
fields.description: The applicant's current nationality, as a short code (see nationalityAtBirth).

fields.name: placeOfBirthVillageTown
fields.label: Place of Birth — Village/Town
fields.description: The village or town where the applicant was born.

fields.name: placeOfBirthRegionCountry
fields.label: Place of Birth — Region/Country
fields.description: The region (if born in Ghana) or country (if born abroad) where the applicant was born, as a short code.

fields.name: placeOfBirthDistrictState
fields.label: Place of Birth — District/State
fields.description: The district (if born in Ghana) or state (if born abroad) where the applicant was born.

fields.name: hometownVillageTown
fields.label: Hometown — Village/Town
fields.description: The applicant's hometown village or town.

fields.name: hometownRegionCountry
fields.label: Hometown — Region/Country
fields.description: The applicant's hometown region or country, as a short code.

fields.name: hometownDistrictState
fields.label: Hometown — District/State
fields.description: The applicant's hometown district or state.

fields.name: occupation
fields.label: OCCUPATION
fields.description: The applicant's occupation. The 4-character-wide combed box indicates a short occupation code is expected rather than a free-text job title (see VERIFICATION.md).

fields.name: residentialVillageTown
fields.label: Residential Address — Village/Town
fields.description: The village or town of the applicant's residential address.

fields.label: Verification Document (Type)

fields.label: Verification Document — Date Issued
fields.description: The date the verification document was issued.

fields.name: verificationDocumentAdditionalNumber
fields.label: Verification Document — Additional Number
fields.description: A second combed text widget positioned directly beneath the "Document No. / NID" field, with no printed label of its own anywhere on the form. Its purpose is undefined by the source; modelled here as an optional field on the same basis as the printed field immediately above it, not asserted to carry any specific meaning (see VERIFICATION.md).
fields.sourceRef: VERIFICATION DOCUMENT (TYPE) — unlabelled widget beneath Document No. / NID

documents.label: Applicant's declaration
documents.statement: I declare that all the information presented for this application is true and correct and that all documents that I have provided for the purposes of this application are genuine. I understand that if any information I have provided for this application is false or incorrect, I will be liable to prosecution in accordance with Section 40 of the National Identity Register Act, 2008 (Act 750) as well as any other law or regulation which may be in force at the time. I understand that the information and documents I have provided in respect of this application are stored and handled by the NIA and I have the right to have them updated should they change. I declare that all the information contained in this application form has been read, interpreted and explained to me in a language I understand and I perfectly understood and approved same before my hand was guided to make my mark.
documents.sourceRef: Page 2, declaration paragraph above APPLICANT'S SIGNATURE OR THUMBPRINT *
